gloss.en: feeling or showing sympathy for others
gloss.zh: 有同情心的；富有怜悯心的

words.example: She is a kind and compassionate person.

gloss.en: able to be used or to exist together

words.example: This software is compatible with most computers.

gloss.en: the act of finishing something

words.example: the completion of the new hospital building

gloss.en: made of many connected parts; hard to understand

words.example: This is a complex problem with no easy answer.

gloss.en: the natural colour and look of your skin

words.example: She has a clear, healthy complexion.

gloss.en: made of many parts; difficult to understand

words.example: The rules of the game are very complicated.

gloss.en: one of the parts that something is made of

words.example: Each component of the machine must work well.

gloss.en: to write music

words.example: Mozart composed his last opera before he died.

gloss.en: a person who writes music

words.example: Verdi was a famous composer of operas.

gloss.en: a thing made of two or more combined parts

words.example: Water is a compound of hydrogen and oxygen.

gloss.en: the ability to understand something

words.example: The test checks your reading comprehension.

gloss.en: including all or almost all details

words.example: The book gives a comprehensive list of addresses.

gloss.en: to press something into a smaller space

words.example: You can compress the file to save space.

gloss.en: the act of pressing something into less space

words.example: Image compression makes the file much smaller.

gloss.en: to consist of or be made up of

words.example: The collection comprises 327 paintings.

gloss.en: required by a law or rule

words.example: English is a compulsory subject at this level.

gloss.en: too much pride in yourself

words.example: His conceit made him hard to like.

gloss.en: possible to imagine or believe

words.example: We tried every conceivable way to fix it.

gloss.en: the ability to focus attention on one thing

words.example: This book requires a great deal of concentration.

gloss.en: an abstract idea or principle

words.example: They studied the concept of social class.

gloss.en: a feeling of worry shared by people

words.example: Villagers expressed concern about the traffic.

gloss.en: worried about something or someone

words.example: Concerned citizens can have a huge impact.

gloss.en: about; relating to

words.example: I have a question concerning your report.

gloss.en: based on facts; real and definite

words.example: We need concrete evidence to prove it.

gloss.en: a person's behaviour in a situation

words.example: The sport has a strict code of conduct.

gloss.en: a person who directs musicians playing music

words.example: The conductor raised his hands and the music began.

gloss.en: a solid shape with a round base and pointed top

words.example: The child ate an ice cream cone.

gloss.en: a large formal meeting for discussion

words.example: The hotel is used for conferences and social events.

gloss.en: holding meetings using technology like video

words.example: The team uses video conferencing to talk daily.

gloss.en: to admit you did something wrong

words.example: After hours of questioning, the suspect confessed.

gloss.en: belief that you can trust or be sure

words.example: The president's actions hardly inspire confidence.

gloss.en: in a way that shows you are sure

words.example: She answered every question confidently.

gloss.en: a serious disagreement or argument

words.example: The violence was the result of political conflicts.

gloss.en: difficult to understand; not clear

words.example: The instructions on the box are very confusing.

gloss.en: a state of not being certain or clear

words.example: The announcement caused a lot of confusion.

gloss.en: a word that joins parts of a sentence

words.example: The word "and" is a conjunction.

gloss.en: taking control of a place by force

words.example: The book is about the conquest of new lands.

gloss.en: aware of something; awake

words.example: She's very conscious of the problems involved.

gloss.en: as a result; therefore

words.example: It rained hard and consequently the game was cancelled.

gloss.en: protection of the natural environment

words.example: She is interested in wildlife conservation.

gloss.en: preferring traditional styles; against sudden change

words.example: He holds the conservative views of his parents.

gloss.en: great in amount, size or importance

words.example: The project wasted a considerable amount of time.

gloss.en: by a large amount; much

words.example: The need for sleep varies considerably from person to person.

gloss.en: careful thought about something

words.example: Please give due consideration to the request.

gloss.en: taking into account; given that

words.example: She did well, considering she was so tired.

gloss.en: always behaving or happening the same way

words.example: They need a consistent approach to the problem.

gloss.en: in the same way every time

words.example: Her work has been of a consistently high standard.

gloss.en: comfort given to someone who is sad

words.example: His kind words were a great consolation to her.

gloss.en: to comfort someone who is sad

words.example: She tried to console her crying friend.

gloss.en: a secret plan to do something harmful

words.example: It was a conspiracy to overthrow the government.

gloss.en: happening all the time or repeatedly

words.example: There were constant interruptions.

gloss.en: all the time; repeatedly

words.example: Fashion is constantly changing.

gloss.en: to build or make something

words.example: The building was constructed in 1993.

gloss.en: the process of building something

words.example: Construction began this year and will take months.

gloss.en: to go to someone for advice or information

words.example: If the pain continues, consult your doctor.

gloss.en: an expert paid to give advice

words.example: They hired a technology consultant.

gloss.en: the amount of energy or goods used

words.example: The country reduced its fuel consumption.

gloss.en: to make something dirty or impure

words.example: The chemicals can contaminate the water.

gloss.en: belonging to the present or same time

words.example: The gallery shows contemporary art.

gloss.en: a competition to win something

words.example: They held a singing contest at school.

gloss.en: an official written agreement

words.example: All employees have a written contract of employment.

gloss.en: to say the opposite of what was said

words.example: His story seemed to contradict the facts.

gloss.en: opposite and unable to both be true

words.example: We received two contradictory reports.

gloss.en: to give money or help toward something

words.example: Many people contribute money to the charity.

gloss.en: a gift or payment to help with something

words.example: All contributions will be gratefully received.

gloss.en: causing strong public disagreement

words.example: It is a highly controversial topic.

gloss.en: public argument about something

words.example: The decision caused a lot of controversy.

gloss.en: the quality of being useful and easy

words.example: We provide seats for the convenience of customers.

gloss.en: in a way that is easy or useful

words.example: The shop is conveniently located near my home.

gloss.en: a usual way of doing something in society

words.example: It is a social convention to shake hands.

gloss.en: following accepted, normal ways

words.example: They chose a conventional way of doing things.

gloss.en: the opposite of something

words.example: The converse of this idea is also true.

gloss.en: in an opposite way; on the other hand

words.example: He loves cold weather; conversely, she hates it.

gloss.en: to change something into another form

words.example: They took nine months to convert the building.

gloss.en: to make ideas or feelings known

words.example: Colours like red convey a sense of energy.

gloss.en: completely sure about something

words.example: Sam nodded but he didn't look convinced.

gloss.en: making someone believe something is true

words.example: She gave a convincing argument for her plan.

gloss.en: working together for a shared goal

words.example: The project needs cooperation between teams.

gloss.en: to deal successfully with something hard

words.example: She found it hard to cope with the stress.

gloss.en: hard material formed by sea animals

words.example: Many fish live around the coral reef.

gloss.en: the central or most important part

words.example: English and math are core subjects.

gloss.en: the most important part of something

words.example: Trust is the cornerstone of any friendship.

gloss.en: a small brass musical instrument like a trumpet

words.example: He played the cornet in the band.

gloss.en: a ring of light around the sun or moon

words.example: The sun's corona is visible during an eclipse.

gloss.en: relating to the human body

words.example: The school banned corporal punishment.

gloss.en: connected with a large business company

words.example: The bank gave advice on corporate finance.

gloss.en: a large business company

words.example: He works for a large corporation.

gloss.en: the quality of being right or accurate

words.example: The teacher checked the correctness of the answers.

gloss.en: a long narrow passage inside a building

words.example: His room is along the corridor.

gloss.en: the feeling of being warm and comfortable

words.example: I love the cosiness of the little room.

gloss.en: clothes worn for a place or period

words.example: The museum had an exhibition of Victorian costumes.

gloss.en: warm, comfortable and pleasant

words.example: We sat in a cosy little room by the fire.

gloss.en: a small house, especially in the country

words.example: They live in a small cottage in the country.

gloss.en: an act of pushing air noisily from your throat

words.example: She has a bad cough and a sore throat.

gloss.en: a group elected to govern an area

words.example: The town council met to discuss the plan.

gloss.en: a person from your own country

words.example: He met a fellow countryman while abroad.

gloss.en: happening across the whole country

words.example: The shop has a countrywide network of stores.

gloss.en: an area with its own local government

words.example: The farm is in a rural county.

gloss.en: the ability to face danger or pain

words.example: He showed great courage and determination.

gloss.en: the reporting of news in the media

words.example: The event got a lot of media coverage.

gloss.en: a person who has no courage

words.example: Don't be a coward; tell her the truth.

gloss.en: showing no courage; afraid

words.example: It was a cowardly act to run away.

gloss.en: the feeling of being warm and comfortable

words.example: The coziness of the cabin made us feel at home.

gloss.en: warm, comfortable and pleasant

words.example: The bedroom is small but very cozy.

gloss.en: a sea animal with a shell and claws

words.example: We caught a crab on the beach.

gloss.en: a thin line where something has broken

words.example: This cup has a crack in it.

gloss.en: a skilled person who makes things by hand

words.example: The table was made by a skilled craftsman.

gloss.en: an accident where a vehicle hits something

words.example: He was hurt in a car crash.

gloss.en: a large hole in the ground or a volcano

words.example: The meteor left a huge crater in the ground.

gloss.en: a line made by folding or pressing

words.example: There is a crease in my shirt.

gloss.en: the act of making something new

words.example: Job creation needs to be the top priority.

gloss.en: in a way that uses new ideas

words.example: She solved the problem creatively.

gloss.en: using imagination to make new things

words.example: Creativity is more important than technical skill.

gloss.en: a living thing that can move around

words.example: We should respect all living creatures.

gloss.en: an arrangement to pay for something later

words.example: She bought the sofa on credit.

gloss.en: a small narrow stream or river

words.example: The children played in the shallow creek.

gloss.en: making you feel nervous or frightened

words.example: The old house felt dark and creepy.

gloss.en: the people working on a ship or plane

words.example: The plane has six crew members.

gloss.en: connected with or involving crime

words.example: He was charged with criminal activities.

gloss.en: a person unable to use part of their body

words.example: The old word "cripple" is now seen as rude.
